methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $27M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Supersonic & Hypersonic (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 2 same-sector peers exist in Supersonic & Hypersonic, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Astro Mechanica, a Series A-stage supersonic and hypersonic technology company founded by Ian Brooke, is evaluated against a narrowly defined comp universe of two public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and two private peers (Boom Supersonic and Hermeus) in the same sector.

The valuation analysis employs a stage-progression methodology, the only applicable triangulation approach given data constraints. Using total capital raised of $27M as a proxy for post-seed valuation, a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement yields an implied valuation of $54M. The peer-median method is not applicable: while two same-sector peers exist, neither the same-stage, adjacent-stage, nor similar-tier filters produced the minimum three datapoints required for reliable median estimation. Applying a narrow-cohort peer multiple would introduce unwarranted precision and risk systematic bias; falling back to all-sector medians would sacrifice sector relevance.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $46M (low) to $62M (high), with a median point estimate of $54M. This range reflects stage-typical step-up dynamics but carries meaningful uncertainty due to reliance on a single method. Key limitations include the absence of comparable-stage private comps and limited public comp applicability to an early-stage private frontier-tech business. Investors should weight this analysis against detailed due diligence on technical progress, capital efficiency, and subsector positioning within supersonic/hypersonic development timelines.
